Loreya brunnescens, like some other members of the Zingiberaceae family, may possess medicinal properties due to its bioactive compounds, but further research is needed to confirm its uses. The plant is native to New Caledonia, and its traditional uses and potential health benefits are not well-documented in scientific literature.
